data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-22T19:04 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.thinking:
Deacon, checking in. I need to follow the startup protocol:

1. Announce: "Deacon, checking in." (ONE line)
2. Then IMMEDIATELY run: `bd show skogtown-wisp-wisp-037x`
3. Begin execution

But wait - the hooked work shows that all children are already completed (✓):
- ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-0z5n: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages
- ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-krhd: Check for orphaned processes


- ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-nkg0: Decide: loop or hand off
- ✓ skogtown-wisp-wisp-zt1g: Scan agent health

I need to run the heartbeat command first, then execute the bead show command for the hooked work ID.  The instructions are clear: announce once, run both commands immediately, and begin execution without waiting.

? skogtown-wisp-wisp-037x [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic

Deacon patrol cycle.

content.thinking: The `bd show` command crashed with a Dolt nil pointer dereference. This might be a wisp database corruption issue. Let me try to just check the current mol status and proceed with the patrol. The new patrol ID is `skogtown-wisp-wisp-qqqqu` but trying to show it caused a panic. Let me try `bd mol current` to see if the new patrol steps are available.

content.text: Dolt crash on the new wisp. This is a known intermittent issue — likely database lock contention. Let me retry.

input.description: Retry showing new patrol wisp after brief wait

content.content:
? skogtown-wisp-wisp-qqqqu [EPIC] · mol-deacon-patrol   [● P2 · HOOKED]
Assignee: deacon · Type: epic
Created: 2026-02-22 · Updated: 2026-02-22

DESCRIPTION
Deacon patrol cycle.

Monitors Gas Town agents (Mayor, Witnesses), processes lifecycle requests,
and handles escalations. Created as wisps (ephemeral, not synced to git).

CHILDREN
  ↳ ○ skogtown-wisp-wisp-1kk85: Decide: loop or hand off ● P2
  ↳ ○ skogtown-wisp-wisp-1ttof: Check inbox for lifecycle requests and messages ● P2
  ↳ ○ skogtown-wisp-wisp-fiuv6: Scan agent health ● P2
  ↳ ○ skogtown-wisp-wisp-g3dun: Check for orphaned processes ● P2

When making commit messages, instead of just giving co-credit to Claude, also give credit to Happy like so:  <main commit message>  Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)  Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com> Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering> --permission-prompt-tool stdio --allowedTools mcp__happy__change_title --mcp-config {"mcpServers":{"happy":{"type":"http","url":"http://127.0.0.1:41793/"}}} --permission-mode default --settings /home/skogix/.happy/tmp/hooks/session-hook-3669469.json --input-format stream-json
